description_long: This object is a large, rectangular hammer with a metallic, silver-gray head and a long, cylindrical handle wrapped in dark brown and white bands. The head features engraved text and symbols, with one side displaying a prominent inscription referencing 'THOR.' The handle is capped with a metallic pommel and has a looped strap attached at the end, likely for carrying or securing the object. The hammer's head has beveled edges and a smooth, matte finish, giving it a robust and solid appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of Norse mythology, specifically Mjolnir, the mythical hammer of Thor. The object appears heavy and is constructed primarily from metal, with some leather or synthetic material on the handle.
description: A large, metallic hammer with a silver-gray head, engraved text, and a long, banded handle with a looped strap. The design is robust and reminiscent of Norse mythology.
description_view_0: The hammer is shown at an angle, highlighting the engraved inscription on the head and the full length of the handle with its looped strap.
description_view_1: A top-down view displays the flat, square face of the hammer's head and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: This angled view reveals the opposite side of the hammer head, showing additional engravings and the full handle.
description_view_3: A side view focuses on the narrow edge of the hammer head and the handle's alignment, with the strap visible at the top.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: No visible buttons, switches, or wires are present; detonation may be triggered by impact or internal mechanism concealed within the hammer head.
